mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.5 percentage points above its trailing 12-month low (a Sahm-rule-style threshold), it signals recession onset, triggering risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4 percent as of t-3mo
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the effect disappears (t-test p > 0.10) once triggered episodes are isolated in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between a high nominal federal funds rate and elevated CPI growth produces a compressed or negative real policy rate signal; when FEDFUNDS growth outpaces CPIAUCSL disinflation, tightening real financial conditions suppress equity returns over the next 20 trading days, whereas the two series moving independently do not produce a reliable directional signal.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate exceeds 2.0 percentage points
failure_condition: Joint real-rate signal produces Rank IC no higher than either single-series proxy alone across a 3-year rolling walk-forward window, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) signal forward-looking consumer and business confidence in demand, boosting SPX forward returns over the next quarter, but only when the federal funds rate is below 3% because high policy rates historically choke off housing-driven demand transmission to equities.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 5% over trailing 3 months while FEDFUNDS below 3.0%
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS below 3%, HOUST increases are not associated with statistically significant positive forward returns (p > 0.10) relative to baseline unconditional distribution.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects continued labor market strength that supports near-term consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing modest positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and forward 5d SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in industrial production (INDPRO), falling below zero year-over-year growth, signals supply-side contraction and impending earnings weakness for cyclical and manufacturing-heavy S&P 500 constituents, driving negative forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following negative INDPRO YoY prints are positive in more than 55% of instances across the walk-forward sample, invalidating the negative directional claim.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) growth combined with a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals synchronized consumer demand weakening, which is not captured by either series individually, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week as markets price in near-term earnings risk.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth negative while UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: Combined RSAFS/UNRATE trigger shows no improvement in Rank IC over the single-series UNRATE-only baseline across a 2-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of above-trend real GDP growth (GDPC1), positive quarterly GDP surprises reinforce risk-on sentiment and elevate S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter, but this relationship reverses in late-cycle regimes where strong growth prompts fears of monetary tightening.
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: The positive GDPC1-return relationship fails to hold (Rank IC < 0.02) in expansion regimes where FEDFUNDS is below 4%, or reverses sign without a corresponding late-cycle tightening explanation.
